name: ETHDenver
location: Denver, Colorado, USA — LVC at the National Western Center / Stockyards Event Center, 4850 National Western Dr, Denver, CO 80216
dates: Main event Feb 18-21, 2026 (some sources cite Feb 17-21); BUIDLWeek + Camp BUIDL Feb 15-17; SporkDAO Community Mountain Retreat Feb 22-27
attendance: Reported 25,000+ participants from 125+ countries (organizer/recap figures); however post-event coverage emphasized a notably smaller, more intimate event than prior years — side events dropped roughly 68-85% (from ~668 in 2025 to ~215 in 2026) and international attendance declined (Lunar New Year timing, industry cooling, competing events).
defi_themes: Stablecoins as payment rails and cross-border infrastructure (legislation, GENIUS-style provisions, institutional adoption)
Institutional DeFi / institutional ETH and Wall Street + Main Street adoption ("less degen, more boardroom")
TradFi moving onto Ethereum infrastructure (tokenized assets, institutional products; JPMorgan, BlackRock attention)
Crypto policy, regulation, market structure, and compliance/AML (SEC and White House engagement)
Decentralized stablecoin design and DeFi resilience to market crashes (Vitalik's framing: benchmark beyond USD, capture-resistant oracles, staking-yield vs stablecoin tension)
Layer-2 consolidation and DeFi liquidity fragmentation (Base cited as a dominant L2 candidate)
AI x DeFi / agentic finance (AI agents transacting on-chain) — dominant cross-cutting theme of the edition

notable_panels.title: The Next Epoch of Ethereum (Vitalik Buterin keynote)
notable_panels.topic: Main-stage keynote on AI's impact on Ethereum, 'perfect markets', disintermediation and decentralized governance; touches on DeFi needing stable money independent of single issuers

notable_panels.title: New France Village — 'The Future of Finance' stage (DeFi Day track)
notable_panels.topic: Dedicated finance stage covering DeFi, TradFi, Stables, Policy, Regs, Compliance/AML, RWA, RealFi, Treasury Cos, Institutional ETH, Exchanges, Main Street and Wall Street adoption

notable_panels.title: Accelerating Your Stablecoin Strategy (New France Village, Day 1, session ~1105)
notable_panels.topic: Stablecoin strategy, adoption and rails (recorded ETHDenver 2026 New France Village session)

notable_panels.title: Day 3 policy / market-structure discussion
notable_panels.topic: Stablecoin provisions, crypto market structure legislation, and a 'rules-first' regulatory approach
notable_panels.speakers: Patrick Witt (White House, President's Council of Advisors for Digital Assets); Hester Peirce (SEC Commissioner)

notable_speakers.name: Hester Peirce
notable_speakers.affiliation: Commissioner, U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)

notable_speakers.name: Patrick Witt
notable_speakers.affiliation: Executive Director, President's Council of Advisors for Digital Assets, The White House

notable_speakers.name: Bo Hines
notable_speakers.affiliation: Strategic Advisor for Digital Assets and U.S. Strategy, Tether

key_takeaways: The edition marked a clear institutional shift in DeFi — 'less degen, more boardroom', with TradFi (JPMorgan, BlackRock attention) and tokenized/RWA products moving onto Ethereum rails.

Stablecoins were the dominant finance narrative: framed as payment infrastructure / cross-border rails rather than trading tools, with heavy policy and compliance focus driven by SEC and White House presence.

Unusually strong public-sector engagement: SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce ('rules-first' approach) and White House digital-assets lead Patrick Witt addressed stablecoin and market-structure policy — reportedly the first sitting SEC Commissioner to speak officially at ETHDenver.
AI x crypto (agentic finance, AI agents transacting on-chain) overshadowed pure DeFi, with the AI/DePIN 'Futurllama' stage drawing the largest crowds.

Vitalik Buterin's keynote 'The Next Epoch of Ethereum' centered on AI and 'perfect markets'; separately he argued DeFi still lacks stable money truly independent of single issuers (need for a non-USD benchmark, capture-resistant oracles, staking-yield vs stablecoin tension) and is rethinking DeFi resilience to market crashes.
Layer-2 consolidation was a recurring DeFi-adjacent theme, with Base frequently cited as a dominant L2 for institutional/on-chain finance.

The conference was smaller and more builder-focused: side events fell sharply (~68-85% vs 2025), BUIDLathon compressed to 4 in-person days with added online stages, and hackathon prize pools shrank dramatically (~$1.03M in 2025 to ~$132K in 2026), with sponsor money concentrating on AI.

Finance content was organized under the 'New France Village' stage ('The Future of Finance') covering DeFi, stablecoins, RWA/RealFi, institutional ETH, treasury companies, exchanges, and regulation/compliance.
